content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission


November 1, 1994

Fran Farina
Director
Monterey Peninsula Water Management District
Post Office Box 22883
Carmel, California 93922

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-94-328

Dear Ms. Farina:

We are responding to your request for assistance under the
conflict-of-interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
"Act").¹ Since you do not have a specific decision pending before
the Monterey Peninsula Water Management District we are providing
you with informal assistance pursuant to Regulation 18329.²

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In
addition, the Commission does not act as the finder of fact in
providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 7.) Further, the Commission's advice is limited to the provisions of
the Act.

You have indicated that your personal residence is located on
the Carmel River and you are concerned whether, as a consequence,
you are disqualified from participating in actions concerning the
Carmel River and decisions regarding any activity on the Carmel
River. In a telephone conversation you stated that there is no
decision pending at the present time although there are ongoing
matters that come up from time to time.

Sections 87100 and 87103 provide that a public official shall
not make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. I-94-328
Page 2


or her official position to influence a governmental decision if
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the
public generally, on the public official or his or her immediate
family or on one of the specified economic interests. Thus, the
facts and circumstances surrounding each decision must be analyzed
to determine your responsibilities under the Act.

The fact that your residence is located on the Carmel River
is not, standing alone, sufficient to conclude that you have a
conflict of interest regarding all matters involving the Carmel
River. When a decision is pending before the Monterey Peninsula
Water Management District you must analyze the reasonably
foreseeable financial effects of the decision on yourself or on
your immediate family or on the economic interests specified in
Section 87103 to determine whether you have a conflict of
interest. I have enclosed a pamphlet "A Guide to the Political
Reform Act of 1974, California's Conflict of Interest Law for
Public Officials" to assist you in making this determination.

Please contact us again if, after reviewing the pamphlet, you
need further assistance or need a determination of your
responsibilities in connection with a specific pending decision.


Sincerely,

Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jeevan Ahuja
Counsel, Legal Division

SGC:JA:ak

Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a public official whose personal residence is located on the Carmel River is disqualified from participating in all decisions concerning the Carmel River under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A public official's residence location on the Carmel River is not, standing alone, sufficient to create a conflict of interest regarding all Carmel River matters. Instead, each pending decision must be analyzed to determine whether it will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the official or their immediate family.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter with no formal Q/A section headers but clear question and conclusion derivable from letter content. Document explicitly states it provides informal assistance under Regulation 18329.2 and does not provide immunity of formal advice. Reference to specific Government Code sections 87100 and 87103 provided.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a public official whose personal residence is located on the Carmel River is disqualified from participating in all decisions concerning the Carmel River under the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.

CONCLUSION: A public official's residence location on the Carmel River is not, standing alone, sufficient to create a conflict of interest regarding all Carmel River matters. Instead, each pending decision must be analyzed to determine whether it will have a reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on the official or their immediate family.

embedding.summary: FPPC informal advice letter addressing whether a Monterey Peninsula Water Management District director with a residence on the Carmel River is disqualified from participating in decisions concerning the Carmel River. The FPPC concluded that each decision requires individual analysis of potential financial effects rather than a blanket disqualification.
